column1: WHAT ARE COLLOCATIONS? | Collocations in the news:✦ *sustain* injuries
column10: *ignore* warning | Notes:✦ 3*heed somebody/something* ➜ to pay careful attention to somebody’s advice or warning
column12: They *failed to heed* the lessons of history.
column13: If only they had heeded his warnings!
column14: Calls for more legislation to protect tenants *were not heeded*.
column16: The government would do well *to heed this advice*.| Girl attacked: Police are hunting for two youths they believe may be responsible for *a brutal attack* on a teenage girl last week. Amy Whitehead was repeatedly punched in the face and *sustained injuries* that needed hospital treatment. A police spokesperson said, 'It is astonishing that an attack like this can happen in daylight, in a busy street. These people *pose a threat* to the public and must be caught.' | GREEN CAMPAIGNERS SAY NO TO CHEAP FLIGHTS: Environmental campaigners yesterday *launched an attack* on airline companies for offering 6*cut-price flights* to popular holiday destinations. One of them told us, 'We have seen how fast the ice caps are melting. If we *ignore these warnings* for the sake of a cheap holiday, our children and grandchildren will suffer.' Holiday makers disagreed. 'The weather here is so miserable, we need to get away!' said one.| Flood drama in local village: Residents in the village of Little Brook *who failed to heed warnings* of flooding found themselves trapped yesterday when more than six inches of rain fell in just three hours. Police had to *divert traffic* after roads were closed by the floods, and many home owners battled to keep the water out of their houses. A woman was taken to hospital with *serious injuries* when a tree fell on the car she was driving. | TRAFFIC THREAT TO SHOPPING STREET: Shopkeepers say that *heavy traffic* 6*is putting off visitors* to the picturesque village of East Chipping. Three hundred people have signed a petition asking for a bypass. 'We want people to enjoy shopping in a pleasant and peaceful environment, said Glyn Evans, owner of a shop selling fishing equipment. 'The current levels of traffic are *a major threat* to our businesses. | Examples:✦ Two youths carried out *a brutal attack* on Amy White house.
column17: She had to go to hospital because of the *injuries* she *sustained*
column18: While the youths who attacked Amy are free, they *pose a threat* to all of us.
column19: Green campaigners *have launched an attack* on companies who encourage people to fly more.
column20: There may be terrible consequences if we *ignore* about global *warning*.
column21: Some people in Little Brook were trapped in their homes because they did not *heed warnings* about the floods.
column22: Police *diverted traffic* around Little Brook because some of the roads were flooded.
column23: When a tree fell on a car in Little Brook, the driver *sustained serious injuries*.
column24: People do not want to shop in streets where there is *heavy traffic*.
column25: Heavy traffic is *a major threat* to shops in East Chipping.
column26: | Emails: I've just heard that there's some office space free near the Victoria Road roundabout. Do you know it? As far as I can see, it's *ideally suited* to our needs, and I know Steve's been *worried sick* about what we're going to do when the new development team starts. The person who told me about it says it's well furnished and* spotlessly clean*. Would you be free to come with me to take a look this afternoon? Cheers
column27: Could we meet some time tomorrow to discuss yesterday's incident with the customer support team? I must say that seeing it splashed all over the local paper was a very *nasty surprise*, as I would have expected you to have reported the matter to me immediately. As a company, we have worked hard to *earn a reputation* for professional service, and I'll be expecting *a full explanation* when we meet. Unless I hear from you, I'll see you at 11 a.m. in my office.
column28: Thanks for your brochure which arrived last week. You certainly offer a *wide range* of training courses, and some of them look ideal for our needs. We always like to make sure that our staff are *highly trained*, and are constantly trying to find ways to *enhance the quality* of our work. Could you ring me to discuss dates and prices, please? Best wishes
column29:
Dear Stephen, Just a note to tell you that I'm seeing Olga tomorrow about the customer support issue. To be honest, I'm not sure she's really up to the job of managing such an important team. She has allowed some very *negative attitudes* to develop, and they don't seem to be able to *seize opportunities* for sales in the way they did when Kelly was in charge. I'm wondering if we should think about making a *fresh start* with a new manager. Best, Camilla |3*Three Adverts*
MOLLY'S MECHANICS:✦ That's understandable - there's nothing worse than the *unpleasant shock* of an enormous bill for repairs you don't understand.
column30: That's why our all-female gang of mechanics has *become well known* for good work and honest prices.
column31: Does your car need servicing? Are you *very anxious* about the cost?
column32: No need to worry any more — we will give you a *complete explanation* of what needs doing to your car and how much it will cost, before we do the work. | JOHN ORAM ✩ LIFE COACH:✦ This can be done by learning to reject *bad ideas about everything* and concentrate on positive thoughts and actions.
column33: How? I can help you unlock your mind and *take the chances* that life offers.
column34: Spend an hour with me and *improve* your life.
column35: So, if you want to leave your problems behind and *start again,* call me today! | Celebration Dining Co:✦ Most importantly, all our chefs *have had a lot of training*, so whatever your event, the food will be delicious.
column36: From dinner for two to a feast for two hundred, we are *the best people* to supply all your catering needs.
column37: And finally, all the food is prepared in our* extremely clean* kitchens and brought direct to you - no fuss, no preparation, no mess.
column38: In addition, there is a *large selection* of menus, so that everyone can find dishes they like.

column1: VERB + NOUN & VERB + ADVERB COLLOCATIONS | Verbs and nouns:✦ If you *restore confidence* in something, you make people trust it again.
column2: If you *assume control* of a situation, you take charge of it.
column3: If someone is *admitted to hospital*, they are taken into hospital for treatment.
column4: If you *raise the alarm*, you tell people that something is wrong.
column5: If someone *resorts to violence*, they start to use violence.
column6: If you *deny responsibility* for something, you say it was not your fault. | 8*Notes*:✦ 3*resort to something* ➜ to do something that you do not want to do because you cannot find any other way of achieving something
column7: to make use of something, especially something bad, as a means of achieving something, often because there is no other possible solution
column9: They felt obliged to *resort to violence*.
column10: They achieved their demands without having to *resort to force*.
column11: We had to *resort to another loan* from the bank.
column12: 3*resort to doing something* ➜ We may have to resort to using untrained staff.
column13: To *restore confidence* means to re-establish trust or belief in oneself, a person, or a system after it has been lost or shaken.
